name: workflow + diagram decisions (ADR index)
description: Agentic context model + living drawio diagram + this workflow (ADR-0014, ADR-0015, ADR-0016). The diagram is a source of truth — update it in the same change as any system-shape change.

content: When recording index/reference mulch records, pass --files explicitly — ml auto-fills files[] from the ENTIRE working-tree changeset when omitted, polluting the record. Also: treehouse.toml post_create must be a TOML array (post_create = ["pnpm install"]); mulch reference records require --name; sd plan step priority must be an integer and 'alternatives' section wants objects not strings.

name: deep-module design tenet (ADR index)
description: Design deep modules: a small interface over a large implementation, at a clean seam, testable through that interface. Vocabulary (module/interface/depth/seam/adapter) + module-depth as a review criterion; operative checklist in docs/conventions.md. Open ADR-0017 before changing.

sd plan submit rejects an 'alternatives' section written as a list of strings — 'sd plan prompt' labels it '(list)' but each item must be an object {name, rejected_because}. Plain strings fail validation with 'sections.alternatives.N must be a object'.
resolution: Author alternatives as objects: [{"name":"...","rejected_because":"..."}]. risks/acceptance remain plain-string lists; only alternatives is object-shaped.

name: same-tree parallel subagents for disjoint doc edits
description: In a /feature Phase-2 wave where the ready seeds edit STRICTLY DISJOINT files and perform NO git/sd/ml ops, fan out same-session subagents in the shared working tree instead of cold treehouse worktrees. The orchestrator holds all VCS/seeds/mulch bookkeeping (claim/close/commit) at the barrier; subagents only edit their owned files and return a structured summary. Cheaper than worktrees for docs-only multi-file changes; treehouse isolation is still required when agents touch overlapping files or run their own gate/push.

content: The living drawio diagram (ADR-0015) shows external-service state through node STYLE: dashed-grey (fillColor=#f5f5f5;strokeColor=#999999;dashed=1;fontColor=#666666) = deferred/stubbed; solid-orange (fillColor=#ffe6cc;strokeColor=#d79b00) = live/active. When a service goes live (ADR-0018: e_resend mail deferred→live; e_gateway now 'Gateway (default) OR direct Anthropic/Voyage by key'), flip BOTH the label AND the style, keep the a_adr node's ADR range current (now 0001–0018), then regenerate deliverables: drawio -x -f svg -e -o resonance-architecture.svg ... and -f png -o resonance-architecture.drawio.png. Validate XML first. drawio CLI is at /opt/homebrew/bin/drawio.
